openmeter/app.purpose: Marketplace registry and runtime lifecycle for installed billing apps (Stripe, Sandbox, CustomInvoicing). Owns the App interface, AppFactory/RegistryItem pattern for pluggable app types, and orchestrates install/uninstall, OAuth2 flows, and customer data delegation to each app type.

patterns.name: AppFactory self-registration at constructor
patterns.description: Each app type's factory registers its MarketplaceListing with app.Service.RegisterMarketplaceListing inside its own New()/NewFactory() constructor, not at wire time.
patterns.example: stripe/service/factory.go: svc.appService.RegisterMarketplaceListing(RegistryItem{Listing: stripeListing, Factory: svc})

patterns.name: App embeds AppBase
patterns.description: Concrete App structs (stripe.App, sandbox.App, custominvoicing.App) embed app.AppBase which provides GetAppBase(), GetID(), GetType(), GetStatus(), ValidateCapabilities() — never duplicate these method implementations.
patterns.example: type App struct { app.AppBase; ... }

patterns.name: Input.Validate() at every entry point
patterns.description: Every service method calls input.Validate() before delegating to adapter or external provider. Every input struct has a Validate() error method that enforces namespace cross-checks, required fields, and namespace equality.
patterns.example: EnsureCustomerInput.Validate() checks AppID.Namespace == CustomerID.Namespace

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion per file
patterns.description: Each file that implements an interface has a package-level var _ <Interface> = (*ConcreteType)(nil) or var _ <Interface> = ConcreteType{} assertion to catch drift at compile time.
patterns.example: var _ app.Service = (*service)(nil)

patterns.name: GetEventAppData for event serialization
patterns.description: App types implement GetEventAppData() (EventAppData, error) to produce a map[string]any for domain events. Use NewEventAppData(v) for serialization; consumers call ParseInto(&target).
patterns.example: app/event.go: NewEventApp(app) calls app.GetEventAppData() to build the EventApp payload

patterns.name: models.Generic* error wrapping for domain errors
patterns.description: All domain errors wrap a models.GenericNotFoundError, GenericValidationError, GenericConflictError, or GenericPreConditionFailedError so the HTTP encoder chain maps them to the correct status code.
patterns.example: errors.go: AppNotFoundError wraps models.NewGenericNotFoundError(...)

key_file_guides.role: Adapter interface definition — the persistence boundary for the app domain. Composes AppAdapter + entutils.TxCreator.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All methods that write state must also appear in entutils.TxCreator chain; never call db directly outside TransactingRepo in the adapter implementation.

key_file_guides.role: App runtime interface + all input/output types for installed-app operations (GetAppInput, UpdateAppInput, CreateAppInput, ListAppInput, UpdateAppStatusInput).
key_file_guides.watch_for: AppConfigUpdate is an open interface — new app types inject a typed config update struct; must call AppConfigUpdate.Validate() inside UpdateAppInput.Validate().

key_file_guides.role: AppBase value type with all shared fields (Type, Status, Listing, Metadata) and canonical implementations of GetAppBase, GetID, GetType, GetStatus, ValidateCapabilities.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add app-type-specific fields here. AppType constants must be kept in sync with Ent schema app_type enum.

key_file_guides.role: AppFactory, AppFactoryInstallWithAPIKey, AppFactoryInstall interfaces and RegistryItem. This is the extension point for new app types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: RegistryItem.Factory must be non-nil; Validate() enforces this. A new app type needs both a Factory implementation and a registered MarketplaceListing.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface (= AppService) — the full public API for the app domain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Marketplace methods (RegisterMarketplaceListing, GetMarketplaceListing) operate on the in-memory registry, not the DB. Installed-app methods (CreateApp, GetApp, UpdateApp) go through the adapter.

key_file_guides.role: Typed domain errors: AppNotFoundError, AppDefaultNotFoundError, AppProviderAuthenticationError, AppProviderError, AppProviderPreConditionError, AppCustomerPreConditionError.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Each error type has a corresponding IsXxxError(err) helper. Always use these typed errors; never return raw fmt.Errorf from service or adapter methods.

key_file_guides.role: Domain event types (AppCreateEvent, AppUpdateEvent, AppDeleteEvent) and EventApp/EventAppData serialization helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AppUpdateEvent is v2 (carries full EventApp); AppCreateEvent is v1 (carries only AppBase). Do not bump version numbers in-place — add a new versioned struct.

anti_patterns: Calling adapter/Ent methods directly inside App method implementations — app-type-specific logic must go through the service layer, not bypass it.
Returning errors without wrapping in a models.Generic* type — HTTP error encoder will fall through to 500 for unrecognized error types.
Constructing a RegistryItem without setting Factory — RegistryItem.Validate() panics and the app cannot be installed.
Adding new AppType constants without updating both AppType.Validate() and the Ent schema app_type enum — causes runtime panics on unknown type.
Publishing events inside the adapter layer — event publishing is exclusively the service layer's responsibility after successful adapter writes.

decisions.decision: Registry (marketplace listings) lives in-memory on the adapter, not in the database.
decisions.rationale: Listings are static metadata provided by factories at startup; they don't need persistence and would add unnecessary DB round-trips for every install call.

decisions.decision: App.GetEventAppData() decouples app-type-specific serialization from the event infrastructure.
decisions.rationale: EventApp is a type-neutral map[string]any so the Watermill bus doesn't need to know about concrete app types; consumers use ParseInto to decode.

decisions.decision: OAuth2 install methods on the adapter return 'not implemented' — only API-key and no-credentials installs are storage-backed.
decisions.rationale: OAuth2 flow state is transient and provider-specific; adapter-level storage would couple the adapter to provider protocols.

code_examples.scenario: Implementing a new app type factory that self-registers

openmeter/customer.purpose: Customer lifecycle management (CRUD, usage attributions, soft-delete) with two extension registries — RequestValidatorRegistry for pre-mutation cross-domain guards and ServiceHooks[Customer] for post-lifecycle callbacks — that avoid circular imports between billing, ledger, and subscription domains.

patterns.name: transaction.Run wraps every mutation
patterns.description: All CreateCustomer, UpdateCustomer, DeleteCustomer calls go through transaction.Run / transaction.RunWithNoValue in service/service.go, ensuring hooks and event publishes are atomic with the DB write.
patterns.example: service/customer.go: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context, tx customer.Adapter) (*Customer, error) { ... })

patterns.name: RequestValidatorRegistry pre-mutation fan-out
patterns.description: Before any DB write, service calls s.requestValidatorRegistry.ValidateXxx(ctx, input) which fans out to all registered validators (billing constraint, entitlement constraint). Errors from multiple validators are joined with errors.Join.
patterns.example: requestvalidator.go: requestValidatorRegistry.ValidateDeleteCustomer fans out to all registered validators

patterns.name: ServiceHooks[Customer] post-lifecycle fan-out
patterns.description: models.ServiceHooks[Customer] is embedded in Service; callers register hooks via RegisterHooks(). Hooks must use NewContextWithSkipSubjectCustomer(ctx) to prevent re-entrant invocations from hooks that call back into customer.Service.
patterns.example: service/service.go: s.hooks.PostCreate(ctx, customer) after successful adapter write

patterns.name: models.Generic* error types for all domain conditions
patterns.description: All customer-specific errors (KeyConflictError, SubjectKeyConflictError, UpdateAfterDeleteError) embed a models.Generic* error so HTTP encoders map them to the correct status code without special-casing.
patterns.example: errors.go: KeyConflictError embeds models.NewGenericConflictError(...)

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapper on every adapter method
patterns.description: adapter/customer.go wraps every method body in entutils.TransactingRepo so the adapter automatically rebinds to any in-flight transaction from ctx, or starts its own.
patterns.example: adapter/customer.go: entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a.db, func(ctx context.Context, tx *entdb.Tx) (*Customer, error) { ... })

patterns.name: Soft-delete pattern via DeletedAt
patterns.description: Customers are never hard-deleted. DeleteCustomer sets DeletedAt; queries default to excluding deleted records (IncludeDeleted=false). Customer.IsDeleted() checks DeletedAt < clock.Now().
patterns.example: customer.go: func (c Customer) IsDeleted() bool { return c.DeletedAt != nil && c.DeletedAt.Before(clock.Now()) }

key_file_guides.role: Service interface: composes CustomerService + RequestValidatorService + models.ServiceHooks[Customer]. This is the full API surface for the customer domain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Service.RegisterHooks and Service.RegisterRequestValidator are both needed — one for lifecycle hooks, one for pre-mutation validation. They are separate registries with separate call sites.

key_file_guides.role: Customer, CustomerMutate, CustomerID, CustomerUsageAttribution domain types and all input types (CreateCustomerInput, UpdateCustomerInput, ListCustomersInput, GetCustomerInput).
key_file_guides.watch_for: CustomerID is a type alias for models.NamespacedID, not a struct embed. GetCustomerInput supports three lookup modes (by ID, by Key, by IDOrKey) — never accept a raw string; always construct one of the typed lookup structs.

key_file_guides.role: RequestValidator interface, NoopRequestValidator, and requestValidatorRegistry with RWMutex fan-out. Validators enforce cross-domain pre-conditions (billing, entitlements) without importing those packages.
key_file_guides.watch_for: validators.Register() is not idempotent — registering the same validator twice doubles validation calls. Wire providers must guard against double-registration.

key_file_guides.role: Typed domain errors: KeyConflictError, SubjectKeyConflictError, UpdateAfterDeleteError, ErrDeletingCustomerWithActiveSubscriptions (as ValidationIssue).
key_file_guides.watch_for: ErrDeletingCustomerWithActiveSubscriptions is a models.ValidationIssue (not a models.GenericError) and carries the list of active subscription IDs in attrs.

key_file_guides.role: Adapter interface: composes CustomerAdapter + entutils.TxCreator. The persistence boundary.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adapter never returns domain events — that is the service layer's responsibility. Adapter methods return typed domain objects (*Customer) not raw Ent rows.

anti_patterns: Calling customer.Service from inside a hook without NewContextWithSkipSubjectCustomer(ctx) — causes infinite re-entrant hook invocations.
Performing DB writes outside transaction.Run / transaction.RunWithNoValue in the service layer — partial writes are not rolled back on hook or publish failure.
Returning raw fmt.Errorf for domain conditions (not-found, conflict, validation) — HTTP encoders depend on models.Generic* typed errors.
Using context.Background() or context.TODO() in service or adapter methods — always propagate caller ctx.
Importing app/common in test files under customer/testutils — causes import cycles; build test deps from package constructors directly.

decisions.decision: RequestValidatorRegistry and ServiceHooks are separate registries with distinct call sites.
decisions.rationale: Pre-mutation validators run before any DB write and block the operation on failure; post-lifecycle hooks run after success and may have side-effects. Mixing them into one registry would lose this timing guarantee.

decisions.decision: Soft-delete rather than hard delete for customers and customer_subjects.
decisions.rationale: Billing, subscription, and entitlement records reference customer IDs and cannot be orphaned. Soft-delete preserves referential integrity while logically removing the customer.

code_examples.scenario: Registering a cross-domain pre-mutation validator that blocks customer deletion when billing records exist

openmeter/entitlement.purpose: Feature entitlement management across three sub-types (metered, boolean, static) with scheduling, override, and supersede lifecycle operations. The metered sub-type drives credit grant burn-down via the credit engine; the service layer composes all sub-types behind a single entitlement.Service interface.

patterns.name: Sub-type connector dispatch via Parser
patterns.description: entitlement.Service dispatches to the correct sub-type connector (metered, boolean, static) using the sub-type discriminator on Entitlement.EntitlementType. The Parser singleton in driver/parser.go is used by HTTP drivers to convert between wire types and domain types.
patterns.example: entitlement/driver/parser.go: Parser.ToAPIGeneric dispatches on EntitlementType

patterns.name: ServiceHooks[Entitlement] fan-out
patterns.description: entitlement.Service embeds models.ServiceHooks[Entitlement]. Other packages (subscription hooks) register hooks via Service.RegisterHooks() to react to entitlement lifecycle without creating circular imports.
patterns.example: entitlement/service/service.go: s.hooks.PostCreate(ctx, entitlement) after creation

patterns.name: transaction.Run for composite mutations
patterns.description: All operations that touch multiple tables (ResetEntitlementUsage, CreateEntitlement with grants, DeleteEntitlement with cleanup) run inside transaction.Run / transaction.RunWithNoValue to ensure atomicity.
patterns.example: entitlement/metered/reset.go: transaction.Run wraps EndCurrentUsagePeriod + balance snapshot + event publish

patterns.name: Distributed lock before per-customer operations
patterns.description: entitlement.Service acquires a pg_advisory_xact_lock via lockr.Locker before operations that modify multiple entitlement rows for the same customer, preventing race conditions in concurrent reset/grant flows.
patterns.example: entitlement/service/lock.go: s.locker.LockForTX(ctx, customerID)

patterns.name: SnapshotEvent V2 for balance change notifications
patterns.description: After any balance change (reset, grant, delete) the metered connector publishes entitlement.SnapshotEvent (v2) to the system Kafka topic. New code must emit V3 events from entitlement/metered/events.go; never create raw SnapshotEvent literals.
patterns.example: entitlement/snapshot/event.go: NewSnapshotEvent constructor with CalculatedAt required

key_file_guides.role: entitlement.Service interface with all 13 lifecycle methods plus models.ServiceHooks[Entitlement] embed. Also defines ListEntitlementsParams and ListEntitlementsWithCustomerResult.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetEntitlementOfCustomerAt resolves by ID first, then by FeatureKey+CustomerID — never pass raw subject keys; always resolve to customer.ID first.

key_file_guides.role: Entitlement domain struct (GenericProperties + sub-type fields), CreateEntitlementInputs, and all validation logic including UsagePeriod minimum 1-hour check.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MeasureUsageFromInput is a value wrapper (not a plain time.Time) to distinguish NOW vs CURRENT_PERIOD_START enums. Always use FromTime or FromEnum, never set ts directly.

key_file_guides.role: EntitlementRepo interface — the persistence boundary for the entitlement domain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EntitlementRepo is intentionally kept separate from entitlement.Service so validators can depend on it without importing the full service graph.

key_file_guides.role: Typed domain errors (AlreadyExistsError, NotFoundError, InvalidValueError, etc.).
key_file_guides.watch_for: AlreadyExistsError wraps models.GenericConflictError — HTTP encoder maps it to 409. Use IsAlreadyExistsError helper for type checking.

anti_patterns: Calling sub-type connector methods directly from HTTP handlers — always go through entitlement.Service.
Constructing CreateEntitlementInputs with ActiveTo set but ActiveFrom not set — Validate() returns an error.
Adding DB queries directly to entitlement.Service struct — all persistence goes through EntitlementRepo.
Using time.Now() instead of clock.Now() in service or adapter methods — breaks test determinism with frozen clocks.
Emitting EntitlementResetEvent (v1) from new code — use EntitlementResetEventV3 from entitlement/metered/events.go.

decisions.decision: Three independent sub-type connectors (metered, boolean, static) composed behind a single entitlement.Service orchestrator.
decisions.rationale: Sub-types have radically different value semantics (credit burn-down vs on/off vs JSON config); keeping them separate prevents the service layer from accumulating type switches.

decisions.decision: Balance worker subscribes to three topics (system, ingest, balance-worker) through a RecalculateEvent intermediary.
decisions.rationale: The two-stage approach (lifecycle event → RecalculateEvent → snapshot) enables deduplication and filter-before-recalculate discipline, preventing redundant ClickHouse queries on burst events.

openmeter/ingest.purpose: CloudEvent ingestion pipeline: the Collector interface receives single events and forwards them to Kafka; DeduplicatingCollector wraps any Collector with Redis or in-memory deduplication; ingestadapter decorates with OTel telemetry. The httpdriver package translates multi-format HTTP requests into ingest.Service calls.

patterns.name: Collector interface wrapping via struct embedding
patterns.description: DeduplicatingCollector embeds Collector and overrides Ingest() to call Deduplicator.IsUnique before delegating to the inner Collector. New decorators must follow the same embed-and-override pattern.
patterns.example: ingest/dedupe.go: DeduplicatingCollector struct embeds Collector and adds Deduplicator field

patterns.name: OTel span per Ingest call in ingestadapter
patterns.description: ingestadapter/telemetry.go wraps every Ingest call with tracer.Start + span.RecordError and increments two counters (events_ingested, errors). Metric instruments are created in the constructor, never inside Ingest().
patterns.example: ingestadapter/telemetry.go: New() creates Int64Counters; Ingest() calls tracer.Start and records span

patterns.name: Topic provisioned on every Ingest call
patterns.description: kafkaingest/collector.go calls TopicResolver.Resolve + TopicProvisioner.EnsureExists on each Ingest, not just at namespace creation. This ensures topics exist even if the namespace was created without Kafka being available.
patterns.example: kafkaingest/collector.go: Ingest calls resolver.Resolve then provisioner.EnsureExists before Producer.Produce

patterns.name: Content-type dispatch in httpdriver
patterns.description: httpdriver/ingest.go decodes requests by Content-Type: application/cloudevents+json (single), application/cloudevents-batch+json (batch). Invalid content types are returned as ErrorInvalidContentType, not raw errors.
patterns.example: httpdriver/ingest.go: switch on r.Header.Get('Content-Type') to select decoder path

key_file_guides.role: Collector interface definition — the only required interface for the ingest domain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Close() is a lifecycle method; callers must call it to drain the Kafka producer before shutdown. Do not add new methods to Collector without considering all implementations.

key_file_guides.role: DeduplicatingCollector — thin deduplication wrapper over any Collector using dedupe.Deduplicator.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IsUnique returns (true, nil) for unique events and (false, nil) for duplicates. A (false, err) case means the deduplicator itself failed — the event is still forwarded to avoid data loss.

key_file_guides.role: ingest.Service wraps Collector with namespace-scoped validation and exposes IngestEvents for multi-event batches.
key_file_guides.watch_for: service.go validates namespace before calling Collector.Ingest. New HTTP endpoints that accept events must go through Service, not Collector directly.

anti_patterns: Calling Producer.Produce in kafkaingest without running KafkaProducerGroup — delivery errors are silently dropped.
Creating OTel metric instruments inside Ingest() instead of in the constructor — causes allocation and registration overhead per event.
Reading namespace from URL path params in httpdriver instead of NamespaceDecoder — bypasses the static namespace injection for self-hosted deployments.
Adding new Kafka headers inside kafkaingest/collector.go without updating the sink worker's header parsing — breaks downstream deserialization.

decisions.decision: OTel telemetry is in a separate ingestadapter package rather than embedded in kafkaingest.
decisions.rationale: Keeps kafkaingest focused on Kafka protocol; ingestadapter can be swapped or omitted in tests without touching producer logic.

openmeter/ledger.purpose: Double-entry ledger for customer financial balances (FBO, Receivable, Accrued) and business accounts (Wash, Earnings, Brokerage). Structured as: domain primitives + routing rules (root), account layer (account/), engine (historical/), transaction templates (transactions/), charge bridge (chargeadapter/), and FBO collector (collector/). When credits.enabled=false, noop/ provides zero-value implementations of all interfaces.

patterns.name: TransactionInput constructed only via transaction templates
patterns.description: All ledger postings are created by calling transactions.ResolveTransactions with typed template structs (CustomerTransactionTemplate, OrgTransactionTemplate). Raw EntryInput/TransactionInput construction outside the transactions/ package breaks sub-account routing.
patterns.example: transactions/resolve.go: ResolveTransactions iterates templates, calls resolve(), then calls ledger.CommitGroup

patterns.name: credits.enabled=false routes to noop/ implementations
patterns.description: app/common wires ledger.Ledger, ledger.AccountResolver, and ledgeraccount.Service to noop/ structs when credits.enabled=false. Every new ledger write path must have a corresponding noop path — enforce via compile-time interface assertions in noop/noop.go.
patterns.example: noop/noop.go: var _ ledger.Ledger = Ledger{}; var _ ledger.AccountResolver = AccountResolver{}

patterns.name: Route → canonical routing key via BuildRoutingKeyV1
patterns.description: SubAccount keys are derived from Route via ledger.BuildRoutingKeyV1(Route{...}). Never manually construct routing key strings — the canonical format normalizes decimals and nil fields.
patterns.example: routing.go: BuildRoutingKeyV1 produces 'currency:USD|tax_code:null|features:null|cost_basis:0.7|credit_priority:null|...'

patterns.name: BookTransaction always inside transaction.Run with ValidateTransactionInputWith
patterns.description: historical/ledger.go calls ValidateTransactionInputWith(ctx, tx, routingValidator) then repo.BookTransaction inside transaction.Run. Calling BookTransaction without the validation step or outside transaction.Run loses the account lock and routing invariants.
patterns.example: historical/ledger.go: transaction.Run(..., func(...) { ValidateTransactionInputWith; repo.BookTransaction })

patterns.name: ChargeAnnotations on every CommitGroup call
patterns.description: chargeadapter handlers annotate every ledger.CommitGroup call with ChargeAnnotations (charge ID, type, direction) via annotations.go helpers. Omitting annotations breaks traceability and correction logic.
patterns.example: chargeadapter/annotations.go: ChargeAnnotations struct; helpers.go sets them on every CommitGroup input

patterns.name: Provisioning lock before EnsureCustomerAccounts
patterns.description: resolvers/account.go acquires a lockr.Locker advisory lock before EnsureCustomerAccounts to prevent duplicate account creation under concurrency. The lock uses a 5-second timeout and converts lockr.ErrLockTimeout to a domain error.
patterns.example: resolvers/account.go: locker.LockForTX(ctx, lockKey) before CreateCustomerAccounts

key_file_guides.role: AccountType constants (FBO, Receivable, Accrued, Wash, Earnings, Brokerage), CustomerAccounts/BusinessAccounts structs, AccountResolver interface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AccountResolver.GetCustomerAccounts panics for unprovisioned customers — always call EnsureCustomerAccounts or check via resolvers before calling GetCustomerAccounts in new code.

key_file_guides.role: Typed account interfaces per account type (CustomerFBOAccount, CustomerReceivableAccount, CustomerAccruedAccount, BusinessAccount) with GetSubAccountForRoute(RouteParams) signatures.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CustomerFBORouteParams requires CreditPriority (non-pointer int). CustomerReceivableRouteParams requires TransactionAuthorizationStatus. Missing required fields will fail Validate().

key_file_guides.role: Route struct, BuildRoutingKeyV1, RoutingKey — the canonical hash key for sub-account routing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CostBasis decimal is canonicalized by trimming trailing zeros before hashing. Always construct Route via typed field assignment, never via string parsing.

key_file_guides.role: ValidateInvariance (debit-credit balance check), ValidateEntryInput, ValidateTransactionInputWith — called before every BookTransaction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ValidateInvariance checks that all entry amounts sum to zero. alpacadecimal addition is used — do not substitute float64 or string parsing.

key_file_guides.role: Zero-value noop implementations of all ledger interfaces used when credits.enabled=false.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every new ledger interface method must be added to the noop with a zero-value return. Missing methods break compile when credits.enabled=false.

anti_patterns: Constructing EntryInput/TransactionInput outside the transactions/ package — bypasses sub-account routing and routing rule validation.
Registering CustomerLedgerHook or namespace ledger handler when credits.enabled=false — these are real DB write paths; noop variants must be used instead.
Calling repo.BookTransaction outside a transaction.Run block — loses the account lock and validation atomicity.
Adding a new transaction template without registering it in the transactionTemplateByName switch in correction.go — the template becomes uncorrectable.
Using context.Background() in any ledger method — breaks OTel tracing and transaction context propagation.

decisions.decision: Transaction templates (transactions/) are the only permitted construction site for EntryInput/TransactionInput.
decisions.rationale: Centralizing construction enforces routing rule compliance and makes corrections (reversals) traceable via annotation-stored template names.

decisions.decision: noop/ package provides compile-time-checked no-ops for all ledger interfaces.
decisions.rationale: Allows the entire billing and customer domain to remain structurally identical regardless of credits.enabled flag — no nil checks needed in business logic.

decisions.decision: Tri-tuple cursor (bookedAt, createdAt, ID) for transaction pagination instead of auto-increment offset.
decisions.rationale: Ledger transactions are append-only but may be inserted out of clock order; a composite cursor with ID tie-breaking provides stable pagination.

code_examples.scenario: Posting a double-entry transaction via templates (the only permitted pattern)
